text: Run `npm ci` in a fresh worktree before any jscpd/node-dependent test or check, or those tests fail spuriously.
The changelog heading date must equal the commit date, or release checks/readback drift.
Run a fresh-clone gate sim before pushing: clone at the to-be-pushed SHA and re-run gates, to catch uncommitted-dependency and tracked-tree drift.
Never trust a gate's process exit code through a pipe; grep the emitted JSON status field. `npm run check | tail` masks npm's exit code.
Duplication findings are line-number-pinned: any edit to a clone file makes the old row stale and creates a new row. Re-anchor duplication rows after touching a cloned file.
Vendored health_common copies must stay byte-identical across leaves; check_vendored_common enforces it. Edit the canonical copy then re-vendor.
Always pass an explicit `--rev` anchor to the diagnosis wave / growth audit so findings and baselines compare against a fixed revision, not a drifting HEAD.

complexity-audit SKILL.md under-/over-specifies the module maintainability-index finding: a pinned model given only the SKILL.md produced 1 row vs 2 expected (missed the module-MI SIMPLIFY, conflated SIMPLIFY/DECOMPOSE).

mine_iteration_kpis._load_baseline_rows counts only dict-shaped baselines; flat-list baselines (instruction_lint_baseline.json, self_audit_baseline.json, security_baseline.json) yield rows_closed=0, under-reporting the headline rows_per_hour. Add isinstance(data,list) handling (key by baseline filename).
promoted: prevented a repeat: re-mined iteration 1 rows_closed 0->19

text: allocate_batches surplus uses per-repo trailing yield from kpis' rows_before/rows_after, but mine_iteration_kpis emits rows_closed (scalar) without per-repo rows_before/after dicts -> allocator yield is always 0 and surplus falls back to first-active. Guaranteed-minimum still holds. Fix: have the miner emit per-repo rows_before/rows_after, or have the allocator read rows_closed.
